briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for May 14, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea's Efforts Towards Carbon Neutrality and Renewable Energy Expansion
sections.content: Today, various stakeholders' efforts towards carbon neutrality and renewable energy expansion in South Korea were actively reported. Gwangmyeong City was recognized for the excellence of its citizen-participatory carbon neutrality policies, winning an award at a national policy case competition, and is expanding its scope to include disaster safety and future library innovation [11, 14, 19, 20]. The Korea Climate Change Research Institute and the Incheon Institute signed a memorandum of understanding for mutual cooperation in responding to climate change, and research and technology development collaborations are also continuing, with Korea Southern Power and Gyeongsang National University successfully hosting a carbon-neutral biochar forum [3, 10, 18]. Furthermore, the Korea Energy Agency and public-private entities are accelerating the spread of renewable energy with the goal of '1,000 Sunlight Income Villages,' and public-led solar power projects utilizing idle land in industrial complexes are also gaining momentum in the Gyeonggi and Chungcheong regions [23, 25, 27, 29, 31, 32]. These efforts are linked to strengthening corporate ESG management and are being attempted in various sectors, such as Shinhan Bank, which is addressing climate issues with new financial risk management standards [4, 13, 16, 17, 21, 24].

sections.title: ⚠️ Deepening Impacts of the Climate Crisis and Social Conflicts
sections.content: Meanwhile, the severe impacts of the climate crisis and the resulting social conflicts are becoming increasingly evident worldwide. Domestically, environmental changes are manifesting in unexpected ways, such as heavy rainfall paradoxically making the land drier, and the global apple industry being shaken by the climate crisis [5, 6]. Internationally, a report indicated that the New Orleans area could become uninhabitable due to climate change, and warnings continued that hundreds of millions in South Asia are exposed to longer and deadlier pre-monsoon heatwaves [42, 45]. Concerns that the 2026 FIFA World Cup could face dangerous heat, along with the concretization of threats to human health and daily life, such as how climate change is aiding the spread of tropical diseases in the U.S., are also emerging [44, 50, 52]. Amidst these circumstances, while opinions suggest that climate change will emerge as a major issue in the 2026 elections, incidents of some politicians mocking students concerned about climate change highlight the social perception gap and conflicts surrounding climate issues [37, 38, 40].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Action and Technological Innovation
sections.content: The international community is exploring diverse approaches and technological innovations to address climate change. The United Arab Emirates (UAE) is poised to become an environmental technology hub combining artificial intelligence (AI) and renewable energy, planning to host the EDMSET event in 2026, while Canada has shown a positive commitment to transition by strengthening its clean energy pledges and announcing plans to lower electricity bills [30, 49]. Scientific advancements, such as the development of new models for predicting plant resistance, are expected to play a crucial role in preparing for climate change [39]. However, the path to energy transition is not always smooth; there was an instance where Germany, despite a surge in heat pump sales, abandoned its 'renewable heating law,' sending mixed signals to Europe's energy transition [33]. Furthermore, competition for critical resources is intensifying, with copper prices reaching an all-time high and Amazon moving to secure mining rights, which is prompting South Korean companies to diversify their strategies through ESG and circular material development [35]. Kerala, India, demonstrated practical environmental protection efforts by establishing eco-friendly election guidelines that prohibit the use of difficult-to-recycle election print materials [36].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Challenges in South Korea's Energy System Transition
sections.content: Despite various positive efforts, South Korea's energy system transition faces structural challenges that need to be addressed. The imbalance where renewable energy facilities are concentrated in rural areas while power consumption is skewed towards the metropolitan area is highlighted as an urgent issue for efficient energy transition [26]. Experts emphasize that simply building more transmission lines is insufficient, and the way the power grid is utilized must fundamentally change [34]. Furthermore, during the energy transition process, such as the closure of coal power plants, workers and citizens are demanding guaranteed total employment and advocating for 'public renewable energy,' indicating the importance of social consensus and policy development for a just transition [28]. Discussions on how to resolve conflicts over 'not-in-my-backyard' (NIMBY) facilities in this era of climate and energy crisis must also be actively pursued [7].

briefing.closing: This concludes today's climate briefing for May 14, 2026. We look forward to bringing you more in-depth news in our next briefing.

articles.description: Demand for AI and power grids drives copper prices to record highs. Amazon is securing mines, while Korean companies focus on ESG and circular materials for copper acquisition. As copper prices surge, global strategies for securing this vital industrial metal are diversifying.

summary.phenomenon: Copper prices have soared to an all-time high, reaching the $14,000 per ton mark. In response, Amazon is actively securing mining rights, while domestic companies are diversifying their copper acquisition strategies through ESG initiatives and the development of circular materials.
summary.cause: Increased demand for AI and power grids has been identified as a primary driver behind the surge in copper prices.
summary.outlook: Should the upward trend in copper prices persist, global corporations are anticipated to explore diverse strategies to secure their copper supplies.

articles.description: India's Kerala state, grappling with waste issues, introduced 'eco-friendly election guidelines' in February, banning hard-to-recycle materials like PVC and polyester for campaign materials and banners, allowing only 100% cotton, paper, and reusable polyethylene. Local governments are leading this green transition.

summary.phenomenon: In February, India's Kerala state established 'eco-friendly election guidelines,' prohibiting the use of difficult-to-recycle PVC and polyester for election print materials and banners, while permitting only 100% cotton, paper, and reusable polyethylene materials.
summary.cause: The local government established these guidelines to spearhead an eco-friendly transition, driven by Kerala state's severe struggle with waste management issues.

articles.description: Groups say supreme court justice, who owns oil stocks, may be violating ethics codes by participating in certain casesThe supreme court justice Samuel Alito, who owns stock in oil companies, may be violating court ethics codes by participating in certain cases that could benefit big oil, government watchdog groups say.In a Thursday letter, a coalition of watchdog organizations called on the Senate judiciary committee to investigate Alito, the sole supreme court justice with holdings in energy co

summary.phenomenon: It has been alleged that Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito may have violated court ethics codes by participating in certain cases that could benefit large oil companies, while owning stock in oil companies.
summary.cause: The cause identified is that Justice Alito's participation in related cases, despite holding stock in energy companies, could create a conflict of interest.
summary.outlook: A coalition of government watchdog groups has urged the Senate Judiciary Committee to investigate Justice Alito.
